classObjective: Identify key features of exponential functions from equations and context.
workedExamples: 8-1, Example 2 — Analyze Exponential Functions in Context (Initial Value, Base, Asymptote)
8-1, Example 3 — Analyze Exponential Functions in Context (Domain, Range)
warmUp: What is the y-intercept of y = 3(2)^x?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: initial value (a) identification; base (b) interpretation; asymptote; domain/range from real-world models
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying key features of exponential functions from equations and real-world contexts
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying initial value, base, asymptote, domain, and range of exponential functions
assessment: Exit ticket: identify the initial value, base, and asymptote of y = 5(0.7)^x and describe what the base tells you about growth or decay
capReflection: How does the value of the base b in y = ab^x indicate whether the function represents growth or decay?
